title: proto.py: Signed int32 fields decoded as unsigned - rx_rssi shows wrong values
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/meshtastic/proto.py
Line: 378 (MeshPacket.from_bytes)

Bug: The _decode_varint() function returns unsigned values, but protobuf int32 fields like rx_rssi encode negative values as 10-byte sign-extended varints. When decoded, negative RSSI values (e.g., -60 dBm) become huge positive numbers (e.g., 18446744073709551556).

Root cause: Line 50-64 _decode_varint() builds an unsigned value. For int32 fields, values > 0x7FFFFFFF should be reinterpreted as signed:
```python
if value > 0x7FFFFFFF:
    value -= 0x100000000
```

Impact: RSSI values displayed in Meshtastic app will be incorrect (huge positive instead of negative). RSSI is typically -30 to -120 dBm.

Fix: Add signed int32 conversion in MeshPacket.from_bytes for rx_rssi field after decoding varint.
